test_cases.description: Verify pyproject.toml declares the correct package name, Python requirement, runtime dependency, and dev/test dependencies.
preconditions: Python 3.12+ and `uv` are available in the environment.
test_cases.input: Parse `packages/sdk-python/pyproject.toml` with a TOML parser.
test_cases.expected: project.name == 'apikit', requires-python == '>=3.12', 'httpx>=0.27' in dependencies, 'respx>=0.21' and 'mypy>=1.10' and 'ruff>=0.4' and 'pytest' in optional-dependencies.dev.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
data = parse_toml('packages/sdk-python/pyproject.toml')
assert data['project']['name'] == 'apikit'
assert data['project']['requires-python'] == '>=3.12'
assert any('httpx' in d for d in data['project']['dependencies'])
dev_deps = data['project']['optional-dependencies']['dev']
assert any('respx' in d for d in dev_deps)
assert any('mypy' in d for d in dev_deps)
assert any('ruff' in d for d in dev_deps)
assert any('pytest' in d for d in dev_deps)

test_cases.description: Verify the package layout includes all required source and test files at the correct paths.
test_cases.input: File system check for existence of each required path.
test_cases.expected: All of `src/apikit/__init__.py`, `src/apikit/client.py`, `src/apikit/models.py`, `src/apikit/exceptions.py`, `tests/__init__.py`, `tests/test_client.py`, `tests/test_models.py`, `tests/test_exceptions.py` exist under `packages/sdk-python/`.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for path in [
  'packages/sdk-python/src/apikit/__init__.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/src/apikit/client.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/src/apikit/models.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/src/apikit/exceptions.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/tests/__init__.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/tests/test_client.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/tests/test_models.py',
  'packages/sdk-python/tests/test_exceptions.py'
]:
  assert file_exists(path)

test_cases.description: Verify that `from apikit import Client, APIError, User, Organization` succeeds and that the exported symbols are the expected types.
preconditions: `packages/sdk-python` is installed or on the Python path.
test_cases.input: Import statement: `from apikit import Client, APIError, User, Organization`.
test_cases.expected: No ImportError is raised; `Client`, `APIError`, `User`, and `Organization` are accessible and are the correct types (class objects).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, APIError, User, Organization
assert isinstance(Client, type)
assert isinstance(APIError, type)
assert isinstance(User, type)
assert isinstance(Organization, type)

test_cases.description: Verify pyproject.toml contains no PyPI publishing configuration such as twine or flit settings.
test_cases.input: Parse `packages/sdk-python/pyproject.toml`.
test_cases.expected: No `[project.urls]` publish entry, no `[tool.flit]`, no `[tool.twine]`, and no build-backend pointing to a publishing tool.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
data = parse_toml('packages/sdk-python/pyproject.toml')
assert 'publish' not in data.get('project', {}).get('urls', {})
assert 'flit' not in data.get('tool', {})
assert 'twine' not in data.get('tool', {})

test_cases.description: Verify the Client constructor accepts base_url (required), mount_point (optional, default '/api/v1'), api_key (optional), and timeout (optional, default 30.0) without raising.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='/api/v1', api_key='tok', timeout=30.0)
test_cases.expected: A `Client` instance is created with no exception; default values are applied when optional args are omitted.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
c1 = Client('https://api.example.com')
assert c1 is not None
c2 = Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='/v2', api_key='tok', timeout=5.0)
assert c2 is not None
# verify defaults
c3 = Client('https://api.example.com')
assert c3._mount_point == '/api/v1'
assert c3._timeout == 30.0

test_cases.description: Verify that trailing slashes on base_url and mount_point are stripped at construction time.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com/', mount_point='/api/v1/')
test_cases.expected: Stored base_url is 'https://api.example.com' and stored mount_point is '/api/v1' with no trailing slash.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
c = Client('https://api.example.com/', mount_point='/api/v1/')
assert c._base_url == 'https://api.example.com'
assert c._mount_point == '/api/v1'

test_cases.description: Verify that healthz, readyz, and version methods send requests to base_url without mount_point, and all other methods use base_url + mount_point.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='/api/v1'); call healthz(), readyz(), version(), and get_user().
test_cases.expected: healthz hits 'https://api.example.com/healthz', readyz hits 'https://api.example.com/readyz', version hits 'https://api.example.com/version', get_user hits 'https://api.example.com/api/v1/user'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route_healthz = respx.get('https://api.example.com/healthz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  route_readyz = respx.get('https://api.example.com/readyz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  route_version = respx.get('https://api.example.com/version').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'version':'1.0','build_time':'t','commit':'c','mount_point':'/api/v1'}))
  route_user = respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='/api/v1', api_key='tok')
  c.healthz(); c.readyz(); c.version(); c.get_user()
  assert route_healthz.called
  assert route_readyz.called
  assert route_version.called
  assert route_user.called

test_cases.description: Verify that the Client forwards the timeout value to the underlying httpx.Client, and that timeout=None disables the timeout.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com', timeout=5.0) and Client('https://api.example.com', timeout=None).
test_cases.expected: The internal httpx.Client is configured with timeout=5.0 in the first case and timeout=None in the second case.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
c1 = Client('https://api.example.com', timeout=5.0)
assert c1._http_client.timeout.connect == 5.0 or c1._http_client.timeout == 5.0
c2 = Client('https://api.example.com', timeout=None)
assert c2._http_client.timeout is None or c2._http_client.timeout == httpx.Timeout(None)

test_cases.description: Verify that Client implements the context manager protocol and that __enter__ returns self and __exit__ closes the underlying httpx.Client.
test_cases.input: `with Client('https://api.example.com') as c:` block that makes no requests.
test_cases.expected: __enter__ returns the Client instance; after the with block, the underlying httpx.Client is closed.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
with Client('https://api.example.com') as c:
  assert c is not None
  inner_http = c._http_client
# after the block
assert inner_http.is_closed

test_cases.description: Verify that __exit__ closes the httpx.Client and does not suppress exceptions raised inside the with block.
test_cases.input: `with Client(...) as c:` block that raises a ValueError.
test_cases.expected: ValueError propagates out of the with block; the underlying httpx.Client is closed.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
caught_exception = None
inner_http = None
try:
  with Client('https://api.example.com') as c:
    inner_http = c._http_client
    raise ValueError('test error')
except ValueError as e:
  caught_exception = e
assert caught_exception is not None
assert str(caught_exception) == 'test error'
assert inner_http.is_closed

test_cases.description: Verify that when api_key is set, every request includes Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='ak_secret'); call get_user().
test_cases.expected: The outgoing GET request has 'Authorization' header equal to 'Bearer ak_secret'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='ak_secret')
  c.get_user()
  assert route.calls.last.request.headers['authorization'] == 'Bearer ak_secret'

test_cases.description: Verify that when api_key is None, no Authorization header is sent on requests.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com', api_key=None); call healthz().
test_cases.expected: The outgoing GET request has no 'Authorization' header.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.get('https://api.example.com/healthz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key=None)
  c.healthz()
  assert 'authorization' not in route.calls.last.request.headers

test_cases.description: Verify that every outgoing request includes Accept: application/json header.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com'); call healthz().
test_cases.expected: The outgoing GET request has 'Accept' header equal to 'application/json'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.get('https://api.example.com/healthz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com')
  c.healthz()
  assert route.calls.last.request.headers['accept'] == 'application/json'

test_cases.description: Verify that POST and PATCH requests include Content-Type: application/json header.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call update_user(full_name='Test').
test_cases.expected: The outgoing PATCH request has 'Content-Type' header equal to 'application/json'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.patch('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  c.update_user(full_name='Test')
  assert 'application/json' in route.calls.last.request.headers['content-type']

test_cases.description: Verify that last_etag and last_request_id are reset to None at the start of each request and then populated from response headers when present.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call get_user() twice: first response includes ETag and X-Request-ID; second response omits them.
test_cases.expected: After first call: last_etag == 'etag-value', last_request_id == 'req-id-value'. After second call: last_etag is None, last_request_id is None.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(side_effect=[
    httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT, headers={'ETag': 'etag-1', 'X-Request-ID': 'req-1'}),
    httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT)
  ])
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  c.get_user()
  assert c.last_etag == 'etag-1'
  assert c.last_request_id == 'req-1'
  c.get_user()
  assert c.last_etag is None
  assert c.last_request_id is None

test_cases.description: Verify that last_etag is None after a response that does not include an ETag header.
test_cases.input: Client; call healthz() with a mocked response that has no ETag header.
test_cases.expected: client.last_etag is None after the call.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/healthz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com')
  c.healthz()
  assert c.last_etag is None

test_cases.description: Verify that last_request_id is None after a response that does not include an X-Request-ID header.
test_cases.input: Client; call healthz() with a mocked response that has no X-Request-ID header.
test_cases.expected: client.last_request_id is None after the call.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/healthz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com')
  c.healthz()
  assert c.last_request_id is None

test_cases.description: Verify that when if_none_match is provided to a conditional GET, the If-None-Match header is sent.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call get_user(if_none_match='"abc123"').
test_cases.expected: The outgoing GET request has 'If-None-Match' header equal to '"abc123"'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  c.get_user(if_none_match='"abc123"')
  assert route.calls.last.request.headers['if-none-match'] == '"abc123"'

test_cases.description: Verify that NotModifiedError is raised when the server returns HTTP 304 to a conditional GET.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call get_user(if_none_match='"etag"') with mocked 304 response.
test_cases.expected: NotModifiedError is raised; it is not an instance of APIError.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, NotModifiedError, APIError
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(304))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  raised = None
  try:
    c.get_user(if_none_match='"etag"')
  except NotModifiedError as e:
    raised = e
  assert raised is not None
  assert not isinstance(raised, APIError)

test_cases.description: Verify that when if_none_match is None, the If-None-Match header is omitted from the request.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call get_user(if_none_match=None).
test_cases.expected: The outgoing GET request has no 'If-None-Match' header.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  c.get_user(if_none_match=None)
  assert 'if-none-match' not in route.calls.last.request.headers

test_cases.description: Verify APIError defines __init__ with code and message, stores both as instance attributes, and is catchable as Exception.
test_cases.input: APIError(code=404, message='not found')
test_cases.expected: e.code == 404, e.message == 'not found', isinstance(e, Exception) is True, str(e) == 'not found'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import APIError
e = APIError(code=404, message='not found')
assert e.code == 404
assert e.message == 'not found'
assert isinstance(e, Exception)
assert str(e) == 'not found'

test_cases.description: Verify that a 4xx response with a valid JSON error envelope raises APIError with code and message from the envelope.
test_cases.input: Mocked 400 response with body '{"error": {"code": 400, "message": "bad request"}}'.
test_cases.expected: APIError is raised with e.code == 400 and e.message == 'bad request'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, APIError
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(400, json={'error': {'code': 400, 'message': 'bad request'}})
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  raised = None
  try:
    c.get_user()
  except APIError as e:
    raised = e
  assert raised is not None
  assert raised.code == 400
  assert raised.message == 'bad request'

test_cases.description: Verify that a 4xx response with a non-JSON body raises APIError with the HTTP status code and raw body text as message.
test_cases.input: Mocked 500 response with plain text body 'Internal Server Error'.
test_cases.expected: APIError is raised with e.code == 500 and e.message == 'Internal Server Error'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, APIError
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(500, text='Internal Server Error')
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  raised = None
  try:
    c.get_user()
  except APIError as e:
    raised = e
  assert raised is not None
  assert raised.code == 500
  assert raised.message == 'Internal Server Error'

test_cases.description: Verify that a 4xx/5xx response with an empty body raises APIError with code set to the HTTP status code and message 'Unexpected error'.
test_cases.input: Mocked 503 response with empty body.
test_cases.expected: APIError raised with e.code == 503 and e.message == 'Unexpected error'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, APIError
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(503, content=b'')
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  raised = None
  try:
    c.get_user()
  except APIError as e:
    raised = e
  assert raised is not None
  assert raised.code == 503
  assert raised.message == 'Unexpected error'

test_cases.description: Verify that UnauthorizedError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, and ConflictError are raised for 401, 403, 404, and 409 responses respectively, and each is an instance of APIError.
test_cases.input: Mocked responses returning 401, 403, 404, and 409 with valid JSON error envelopes.
test_cases.expected: Each raises its specific subclass; each is also an instance of APIError.

test_cases.description: Verify that NotModifiedError is a direct subclass of Exception and not of APIError.
test_cases.input: Inspect NotModifiedError class hierarchy.
test_cases.expected: issubclass(NotModifiedError, Exception) is True; issubclass(NotModifiedError, APIError) is False.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import NotModifiedError, APIError
assert issubclass(NotModifiedError, Exception)
assert not issubclass(NotModifiedError, APIError)
e = NotModifiedError()
assert not isinstance(e, APIError)

test_cases.description: Verify that all ten response dataclasses (User, APIKey, APIKeyWithSecret, PAT, PATWithSecret, Organization, OAuthProvider, AuthCallbackResponse, VersionInfo, HealthStatus) are importable and are dataclasses.
test_cases.input: Import all response dataclasses from apikit.
test_cases.expected: All ten classes exist, are dataclasses (dataclasses.is_dataclass returns True), and are importable without error.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import dataclasses
from apikit import User, APIKey, APIKeyWithSecret, PAT, PATWithSecret, Organization, OAuthProvider, AuthCallbackResponse, VersionInfo, HealthStatus
for cls in [User, APIKey, APIKeyWithSecret, PAT, PATWithSecret, Organization, OAuthProvider, AuthCallbackResponse, VersionInfo, HealthStatus]:
  assert dataclasses.is_dataclass(cls)

test_cases.description: Verify that dataclass field names use snake_case matching JSON keys (e.g., key_id, created_at, provider_id).
test_cases.input: Inspect field names of User, APIKey, Organization, and PAT dataclasses.
test_cases.expected: User has field 'created_at', APIKey has field 'key_id', Organization has field 'created_at', PAT has field 'token_id'.

test_cases.description: Verify that status on User and Organization, and role on User, are typed as plain str without enum enforcement.
test_cases.input: Inspect type annotations of User.status, User.role, Organization.status.
test_cases.expected: All three fields have annotation str (not an Enum subclass); arbitrary string values are accepted in from_dict.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import dataclasses
from apikit import User, Organization
user_hints = {f.name: f.type for f in dataclasses.fields(User)}
assert user_hints['status'] == str or user_hints['status'] is str
assert user_hints['role'] == str or user_hints['role'] is str
org_hints = {f.name: f.type for f in dataclasses.fields(Organization)}
assert org_hints['status'] == str or org_hints['status'] is str
# Arbitrary value accepted
u = User.from_dict({**SAMPLE_USER_DICT, 'status': 'custom_value', 'role': 'superuser'})
assert u.status == 'custom_value'
assert u.role == 'superuser'

test_cases.description: Verify that APIKeyWithSecret has exactly the fields key, key_id, and expires_at, with created_at and revoked_at absent.
test_cases.input: Inspect fields of APIKeyWithSecret dataclass.
test_cases.expected: APIKeyWithSecret has exactly three fields: key (str), key_id (str), expires_at (datetime | None).

test_cases.description: Verify that AuthCallbackResponse has fields user: User and api_key: APIKeyWithSecret.
test_cases.input: Inspect fields of AuthCallbackResponse dataclass.
test_cases.expected: AuthCallbackResponse has fields 'user' and 'api_key'; their types are User and APIKeyWithSecret respectively.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import dataclasses
from apikit import AuthCallbackResponse, User, APIKeyWithSecret
fields = {f.name: f for f in dataclasses.fields(AuthCallbackResponse)}
assert 'user' in fields
assert 'api_key' in fields
assert fields['user'].type is User or fields['user'].type == 'User'
assert fields['api_key'].type is APIKeyWithSecret or fields['api_key'].type == 'APIKeyWithSecret'

test_cases.description: Verify that list_org_members returns list[User] and that no OrgMember type is defined in apikit.
test_cases.input: Call list_org_members with mocked response returning a JSON array of user dicts.
test_cases.expected: Return value is a list of User instances; `from apikit import OrgMember` raises ImportError.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, User
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/org-1/members').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=[SAMPLE_USER_DICT])
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  members = c.list_org_members('org-1')
  assert isinstance(members, list)
  assert all(isinstance(m, User) for m in members)
try:
  from apikit import OrgMember
  assert False, 'should have raised ImportError'
except ImportError:
  pass

test_cases.description: Verify that from_dict on a response dataclass correctly parses RFC 3339 datetime strings into datetime objects and constructs nested dataclass instances.
test_cases.input: User.from_dict({'id':'u1','username':'alice','email':'a@b.com','full_name':None,'status':'active','role':'user','provider':'github','provider_id':'gh-1','created_at':'2024-01-15T12:00:00Z','updated_at':'2024-06-01T00:00:00Z'})
test_cases.expected: Returns a User instance with created_at and updated_at as datetime objects (timezone-aware or naive UTC).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from datetime import datetime
from apikit import User
u = User.from_dict({'id':'u1','username':'alice','email':'a@b.com','full_name':None,'status':'active','role':'user','provider':'github','provider_id':'gh-1','created_at':'2024-01-15T12:00:00Z','updated_at':'2024-06-01T00:00:00Z'})
assert isinstance(u.created_at, datetime)
assert isinstance(u.updated_at, datetime)
assert u.id == 'u1'
assert u.username == 'alice'

test_cases.description: Verify that from_dict silently ignores unknown/extra keys not defined on the dataclass.
test_cases.input: HealthStatus.from_dict({'status': 'ok', 'unknown_future_field': 'some_value', 'another_new_field': 42})
test_cases.expected: Returns HealthStatus(status='ok') without raising any exception.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import HealthStatus
result = HealthStatus.from_dict({'status': 'ok', 'unknown_future_field': 'some_value', 'another_new_field': 42})
assert result.status == 'ok'

test_cases.description: Verify that to_dict on a request dataclass omits optional fields that are None and returns a dict suitable for JSON encoding.
preconditions: `apikit` package is importable (internal request dataclasses accessible).
test_cases.input: UpdateOrgRequest(name=None, url='https://example.com').to_dict()
test_cases.expected: Returns {'url': 'https://example.com'} without a 'name' key.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit.models import UpdateOrgRequest
result = UpdateOrgRequest(name=None, url='https://example.com').to_dict()
assert 'name' not in result
assert result.get('url') == 'https://example.com'
assert None not in result.values()

test_cases.description: Verify that all six internal request dataclasses are defined and pass mypy strict mode.
test_cases.input: Import UpdateUserRequest, CreateUserRequest, CreateTokenRequest, CreateOrgRequest, UpdateOrgRequest, AuthCallbackRequest from apikit.models.
test_cases.expected: All six classes are importable as dataclasses; `uv run mypy .` exits with code 0.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import dataclasses
from apikit.models import UpdateUserRequest, CreateUserRequest, CreateTokenRequest, CreateOrgRequest, UpdateOrgRequest, AuthCallbackRequest
for cls in [UpdateUserRequest, CreateUserRequest, CreateTokenRequest, CreateOrgRequest, UpdateOrgRequest, AuthCallbackRequest]:
  assert dataclasses.is_dataclass(cls)
# None of these appear in public Client method signatures (inspected via inspect.signature)

test_cases.description: Verify that CreateTokenRequest and AuthCallbackRequest have expires field typed as int with default value 90.
test_cases.input: Inspect fields of CreateTokenRequest and AuthCallbackRequest.
test_cases.expected: Both have 'expires' field of type int with default 90.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import dataclasses
from apikit.models import CreateTokenRequest, AuthCallbackRequest
for cls in [CreateTokenRequest, AuthCallbackRequest]:
  fields = {f.name: f for f in dataclasses.fields(cls)}
  assert 'expires' in fields
  assert fields['expires'].default == 90
  instance = cls(**{f.name: f.default if f.default is not dataclasses.MISSING else ('x' if f.type == str else []) for f in dataclasses.fields(cls)})
  assert instance.expires == 90

test_cases.description: Verify that healthz(), readyz(), and version() methods send GET to base_url (without mount_point) and return the correct typed objects.
test_cases.input: Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='/api/v1'); call healthz(), readyz(), version().
test_cases.expected: healthz() returns HealthStatus(status='ok'), readyz() returns HealthStatus(status='ok'), version() returns VersionInfo with correct fields; all URLs hit base_url directly.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, HealthStatus, VersionInfo
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/healthz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/readyz').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'status':'ok'}))
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/version').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'version':'1.0','build_time':'t','commit':'c','mount_point':'/api/v1'}))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='/api/v1')
  h = c.healthz(); assert isinstance(h, HealthStatus); assert h.status == 'ok'
  r = c.readyz(); assert isinstance(r, HealthStatus); assert r.status == 'ok'
  v = c.version(); assert isinstance(v, VersionInfo); assert v.version == '1.0'

test_cases.description: Verify that get_providers() sends GET to mount_point + /auth/providers and returns a list of OAuthProvider instances.
test_cases.input: Client; call get_providers().
test_cases.expected: Returns a list of OAuthProvider instances on HTTP 200.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, OAuthProvider
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/auth/providers').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=[{'name':'github','authorize_url':'https://github.com/login/oauth/authorize'}])
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  providers = c.get_providers()
  assert isinstance(providers, list)
  assert len(providers) == 1
  assert isinstance(providers[0], OAuthProvider)
  assert providers[0].name == 'github'

test_cases.description: Verify that exchange_oauth_code() sends POST to mount_point + /auth/callback with correct body and returns AuthCallbackResponse.
test_cases.input: Client; call exchange_oauth_code(provider='github', code='abc', redirect_uri='https://app.example.com/cb', expires=30).
test_cases.expected: POST is sent to '/api/v1/auth/callback'; body contains provider, code, redirect_uri, expires=30; returns AuthCallbackResponse with nested User and APIKeyWithSecret.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import json
from apikit import Client, AuthCallbackResponse
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.post('https://api.example.com/api/v1/auth/callback').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'user': SAMPLE_USER_DICT, 'api_key': SAMPLE_API_KEY_WITH_SECRET_DICT})
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  result = c.exchange_oauth_code(provider='github', code='abc', redirect_uri='https://app.example.com/cb', expires=30)
  assert isinstance(result, AuthCallbackResponse)
  body = json.loads(route.calls.last.request.content)
  assert body['provider'] == 'github'
  assert body['code'] == 'abc'
  assert body['redirect_uri'] == 'https://app.example.com/cb'
  assert body['expires'] == 30

test_cases.description: Verify get_user() returns User on 200 and raises NotModifiedError on 304.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call get_user() then get_user(if_none_match='etag') with mocked 200 then 304.
test_cases.expected: First call returns User instance; second call raises NotModifiedError.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, User, NotModifiedError
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(side_effect=[
    httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT),
    httpx.Response(304)
  ])
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  u = c.get_user()
  assert isinstance(u, User)
  raised = None
  try:
    c.get_user(if_none_match='etag')
  except NotModifiedError as e:
    raised = e
  assert raised is not None

  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')

  )

  result = c.create_token(name='my-token', permissions=['read'], expires=30)

  assert body['name'] == 'my-token'
  assert body['permissions'] == ['read']
  assert body['expires'] == 30

test_cases.description: Verify list_users() sends GET to /users and appends include_blocked=true query param only when True.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call list_users() and list_users(include_blocked=True).
test_cases.expected: First call URL has no include_blocked param; second call URL includes include_blocked=true.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
with respx.mock:
  route1 = respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/users').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=[SAMPLE_USER_DICT]))
  route2 = respx.get(url__regex=r'.*include_blocked=true.*').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=[SAMPLE_USER_DICT]))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  c.list_users()
  assert 'include_blocked' not in str(route1.calls.last.request.url)
  c.list_users(include_blocked=True)
  assert 'include_blocked=true' in str(route2.calls.last.request.url)

test_cases.description: Verify promote_user, demote_user, block_user, and unblock_user each send POST to correct path and return User on 200.
test_cases.input: Client with api_key; call promote_user('u1'), demote_user('u1'), block_user('u1'), unblock_user('u1').
test_cases.expected: Each sends POST to the correct path suffix (/promote, /demote, /block, /unblock) and returns User.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, User
for action in ['promote', 'demote', 'block', 'unblock']:
  with respx.mock:
    route = respx.post(f'https://api.example.com/api/v1/users/u1/{action}').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
    c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
    method = getattr(c, f'{action}_user')
    result = method('u1')
    assert isinstance(result, User)
    assert route.called

from apikit import Client, Organization

  respx.delete('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/org-1').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(204))
  respx.post('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/org-1/block').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_ORG_DICT))
  respx.post('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/org-1/unblock').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_ORG_DICT))

  assert isinstance(c.block_org('org-1'), Organization)
  assert isinstance(c.unblock_org('org-1'), Organization)

  respx.put('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/org-1/members/u-1').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(204))
  respx.delete('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/org-1/members/u-1').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(204))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')

  assert c.add_org_member('org-1', 'u-1') is None
  assert c.remove_org_member('org-1', 'u-1') is None

test_cases.description: Verify that HTTP 200 and 201 responses are parsed into the documented return type.
test_cases.input: Mocked 200 response for get_user() and mocked 201 response for create_user().
test_cases.expected: get_user() returns User; create_user() returns User parsed from 201 response body.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, User
with respx.mock:
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  respx.post('https://api.example.com/api/v1/users').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(201, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  assert isinstance(c.get_user(), User)
  assert isinstance(c.create_user(username='a', email='a@b.com', provider='p', provider_id='p1'), User)

test_cases.description: Verify that DELETE and PUT endpoints returning 204 return None without parsing body.
test_cases.input: Mocked 204 responses for revoke_key and add_org_member.
test_cases.expected: Both return None.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
with respx.mock:
  respx.delete('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user/keys/k1').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(204))
  respx.put('https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/o1/members/u1').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(204))
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  assert c.revoke_key('k1') is None
  assert c.add_org_member('o1', 'u1') is None

test_cases.description: Verify that refresh_key parses HTTP 200 response as APIKeyWithSecret (not treating it as 201 Created).
test_cases.input: Mocked POST /user/keys/k1/refresh returning HTTP 200 with APIKeyWithSecret JSON.
test_cases.expected: refresh_key returns an APIKeyWithSecret instance; the result lacks created_at and revoked_at fields.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
import dataclasses
from apikit import Client, APIKeyWithSecret
with respx.mock:
  respx.post('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user/keys/k1/refresh').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(200, json={'key':'sec','key_id':'k1','expires_at':None})
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  result = c.refresh_key('k1')
  assert isinstance(result, APIKeyWithSecret)
  field_names = {f.name for f in dataclasses.fields(result)}
  assert 'created_at' not in field_names
  assert 'revoked_at' not in field_names

test_cases.description: Verify that public Client methods with body parameters enforce keyword-only arguments (raise TypeError on positional).
test_cases.input: Call update_user('Alice Smith') with a positional argument.
test_cases.expected: TypeError is raised because full_name is keyword-only.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
import pytest
c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
try:
  c.update_user('Alice Smith')  # positional arg
  assert False, 'Should have raised TypeError'
except TypeError:
  pass

test_cases.description: Verify that all tests pass when running `uv run pytest -q` from the sdk-python directory.
preconditions: All source files are in place.
test_cases.input: Shell command: `cd packages/sdk-python && uv run pytest -q`
test_cases.expected: pytest exits with code 0 and all tests pass.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'pytest', '-q'], cwd='packages/sdk-python', capture_output=True)
assert result.returncode == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that `uv run ruff check .` passes with no errors from the sdk-python directory.
test_cases.input: Shell command: `cd packages/sdk-python && uv run ruff check .`
test_cases.expected: ruff exits with code 0 and reports no errors.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'ruff', 'check', '.'], cwd='packages/sdk-python', capture_output=True)
assert result.returncode == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that `uv run mypy .` passes in strict mode with no errors from the sdk-python directory.
test_cases.input: Shell command: `cd packages/sdk-python && uv run mypy .`
test_cases.expected: mypy exits with code 0 and reports no errors.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = subprocess.run(['uv', 'run', 'mypy', '.'], cwd='packages/sdk-python', capture_output=True)
assert result.returncode == 0

test_cases.description: Verify that the test suite covers all documented scenarios including URL construction, headers, serialization, deserialization, error handling, conditional GET, context manager, and refresh_key 200 parsing.
test_cases.input: Inspect test files for presence of tests covering each documented category.
test_cases.expected: Each of the listed categories has at least one pytest test function.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
# Verified via presence of tests that cover:
# 1. URL construction (base_url-only vs mount_point)
# 2. Trailing slash normalization
# 3. Authorization header presence/absence
# 4. Content-Type and Accept headers
# 5. Request body serialization (datetime, optional fields omitted)
# 6. Response deserialization with datetime parsing
# 7. from_dict ignores unknown fields
# 8. APIError on 4xx/5xx with valid envelope
# 9. APIError on non-JSON body
# 10. APIError on empty body ('Unexpected error')
# 11. UnauthorizedError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, ConflictError subclasses
# 12. NotModifiedError on 304
# 13. last_etag and last_request_id populated/reset
# 14. Context manager closes httpx.Client without suppressing exceptions
# 15. timeout forwarding
# 16. refresh_key parses 200 as APIKeyWithSecret
assert all_categories_covered_in_test_files('packages/sdk-python/tests/')

property_tests.description: For any base_url and mount_point with or without trailing slashes, no constructed URL contains double slashes in the path after the scheme.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary strings for base_url (including with 0-3 trailing slashes) and mount_point (with 0-3 trailing/leading slashes) using hypothesis strategies: base_url drawn from ['https://api.example.com', 'https://api.example.com/', 'https://api.example.com///']; mount_point drawn from ['', '/', '/api/v1', '/api/v1/', '/v2//'].

property_tests.description: For any non-empty mount_point, URLs for healthz, readyz, and version never contain the mount_point string in the path portion.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate non-empty mount_point strings from ['/api/v1', '/v2', '/api', '/mount'] using hypothesis or parametrize; base_url is fixed as 'https://api.example.com'.

property_tests.description: For any sequence of Client method calls, last_etag and last_request_id are always None at the start of each request before response headers are read.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Parametrize over sequences of 2-5 calls to methods including get_user, healthz, list_keys; alternate responses with and without ETag/X-Request-ID headers.
property_tests.invariant_check:
with respx.mock:
  # first response includes ETag, second does not
  respx.get(url__regex='.*').mock(side_effect=[
    httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_DATA, headers={'ETag': 'etag-1', 'X-Request-ID': 'req-1'}),
    httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_DATA),
    httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_DATA, headers={'ETag': 'etag-2'}),
  ])
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  # Monkeypatch _send to capture last_etag at request START before response
  # Assert that at the beginning of each call, both properties equal None
  # This is verified by inspecting the reset behavior in the implementation

property_tests.description: For any dict with arbitrary additional keys passed to any dataclass from_dict, no exception is raised due to unknown keys.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each response dataclass [HealthStatus, VersionInfo, OAuthProvider, APIKey, APIKeyWithSecret, PAT, PATWithSecret, User, Organization, AuthCallbackResponse]: generate valid base dicts and append random additional keys with arbitrary values.

property_tests.description: For any internal request dataclass instance with some optional fields set to None, to_dict() returns a dict with no None values.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each request dataclass [UpdateOrgRequest, CreateOrgRequest, AuthCallbackRequest, CreateTokenRequest]: generate instances with all combinations of optional fields set to None vs a non-None value.

property_tests.description: For any 401, 403, 404, or 409 response, the raised exception is both an instance of its specific subclass and an instance of APIError.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Parametrize over status codes [401, 403, 404, 409] mapped to [UnauthorizedError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, ConflictError] with valid JSON error envelopes.

property_tests.description: For any 304 response to a conditional GET, the raised NotModifiedError is never an instance of APIError.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Parametrize over all conditional GET methods [get_user, get_user_by_id, get_org, get_token] with a mocked 304 response.
property_tests.invariant_check:
for method_name, args, kwargs in [
  ('get_user', [], {'if_none_match': 'etag'}),
  ('get_user_by_id', ['u1'], {'if_none_match': 'etag'}),
  ('get_org', ['o1'], {'if_none_match': 'etag'}),
  ('get_token', ['t1'], {'if_none_match': 'etag'}),
]:
  with respx.mock:
    respx.get(url__regex='.*').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(304))
    c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
    try:
      getattr(c, method_name)(*args, **kwargs)
    except NotModifiedError as e:
      assert not isinstance(e, APIError)
    except Exception:
      pass  # unexpected; let test framework catch it

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that mypy reports an error when the runtime Python version is below 3.12 as declared in requires-python.
preconditions: A Python version < 3.12 is available in the environment.
edge_case_tests.input: Run mypy with Python < 3.12 against the apikit package.
edge_case_tests.expected: mypy exits with non-zero status and reports a Python version incompatibility error.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
result = subprocess.run(['python3.11', '-m', 'mypy', '.'], cwd='packages/sdk-python', capture_output=True)
assert result.returncode != 0
assert 'python_requires' in result.stderr.decode() or 'version' in result.stderr.decode().lower()

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that multiple trailing slashes on base_url are all stripped so no double slashes appear in constructed URLs.
edge_case_tests.expected: Stored base_url is 'https://api.example.com'; all constructed URLs contain no double slashes after the scheme.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
c = Client('https://api.example.com///', mount_point='/api/v1')
assert c._base_url == 'https://api.example.com'
assert '//' not in c._base_url.replace('https://', '')
assert '//' not in (c._base_url + c._mount_point + '/user').replace('https://', '')

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that an empty string mount_point is stored as-is and URL construction remains correct.
edge_case_tests.expected: mount_point is stored as '' and non-health endpoint URLs are constructed as base_url + path (no double slashes).
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client
c = Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='')
assert c._mount_point == ''
# URL construction for /user should be 'https://api.example.com/user'
with respx.mock:
  route = respx.get('https://api.example.com/user').mock(return_value=httpx.Response(200, json=SAMPLE_USER_DICT))
  c2 = Client('https://api.example.com', mount_point='', api_key='tok')
  c2.get_user()
  assert route.called

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that if closing the underlying httpx.Client raises an exception, that exception propagates without suppression.
preconditions: Ability to patch httpx.Client.close to raise an exception.
edge_case_tests.input: Client context manager where httpx.Client.close() raises RuntimeError.
edge_case_tests.expected: RuntimeError propagates out of the __exit__ call; no suppression occurs.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
from unittest.mock import patch
from apikit import Client
raised = None
try:
  with patch('httpx.Client.close', side_effect=RuntimeError('close failed')):
    with Client('https://api.example.com') as c:
      pass  # no exception inside block
except RuntimeError as e:
  raised = e
assert raised is not None
assert str(raised) == 'close failed'

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that a 5xx response with a JSON body missing code or message sub-fields falls back to APIError with HTTP status code and raw response text.
edge_case_tests.input: Mocked 500 response with JSON body '{"error": {"detail": "missing fields"}}'.
edge_case_tests.expected: APIError raised with code=500 and message equals the raw response text; no KeyError or AttributeError propagates.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import Client, APIError
with respx.mock:
  body_text = '{"error": {"detail": "missing fields"}}'
  respx.get('https://api.example.com/api/v1/user').mock(
    return_value=httpx.Response(500, text=body_text)
  )
  c = Client('https://api.example.com', api_key='tok')
  raised = None
  try:
    c.get_user()
  except APIError as e:
    raised = e
  assert raised is not None
  assert raised.code == 500
  assert raised.message == body_text

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that from_dict raises ValueError when a required datetime field contains a malformed string.
edge_case_tests.expected: ValueError is raised; no partial User instance is returned.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import User
raised = None
try:
  User.from_dict({'id':'u1','username':'alice','email':'a@b.com','full_name':None,'status':'active','role':'user','provider':'github','provider_id':'gh-1','created_at':'not-a-date','updated_at':'2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'})
except ValueError as e:
  raised = e
assert raised is not None

edge_case_tests.description: Verify that from_dict raises KeyError or TypeError when a required non-optional field is missing from the input dict.
edge_case_tests.input: User.from_dict({'username':'alice'}) — missing id, email, status, role, provider, provider_id, created_at, updated_at.
edge_case_tests.expected: KeyError or TypeError is raised; no exception masking occurs.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
from apikit import User
raised = None
try:
  User.from_dict({'username': 'alice'})
except (KeyError, TypeError) as e:
  raised = e
assert raised is not None

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the full successful authenticated GET with ETag caching path: construct client, send GET /user, receive 200 with ETag and X-Request-ID, deserialize User, and verify last_etag is populated.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer instantiates Client with base_url, mount_point, api_key (trailing slashes on both), then calls get_user().
expected_effects: Request includes Authorization: Bearer <api_key> and Accept: application/json headers.
last_etag is set to the ETag header value from the response.
last_request_id is set to the X-Request-ID header value from the response.
Return value is a User instance with correctly parsed datetime fields.

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the API error response path: call get_user_by_id for a non-existent user, receive 404 with JSON error envelope, verify NotFoundError is raised with correct code and message.
mockable: httpx transport layer (via respx.mock returning 404 with JSON error envelope)
expected_effects: NotFoundError is raised with code=404 and message='user not found'.
NotFoundError is an instance of APIError.

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the conditional GET raising NotModifiedError path: call get_org with if_none_match, server returns 304, verify NotModifiedError raised.
expected_effects: GET request is sent to 'https://api.example.com/api/v1/orgs/{id}' with If-None-Match header.

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for context manager with exception propagation: open Client as context manager, raise an exception inside, verify httpx.Client is closed and the exception propagates.
smoke_tests.trigger: Developer opens `with Client(base_url, api_key=key) as client:` and raises ValueError inside.
expected_effects: ValueError propagates out of the with block unchanged.
__exit__ returns None (does not suppress the exception).

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for the OAuth code exchange end-to-end path: call exchange_oauth_code with provider, code, redirect_uri, and expires; verify POST body, Content-Type header, and deserialized AuthCallbackResponse.
mockable: httpx transport layer (via respx.mock returning 200 with AuthCallbackResponse JSON)
expected_effects: Request body contains provider='github', code='abc', redirect_uri='https://app.example.com/cb', expires=30.
Return value is AuthCallbackResponse with .user as User and .api_key as APIKeyWithSecret.

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test for health probe URL resolution with trailing slashes: construct Client with trailing slashes on base_url and mount_point, call healthz(), verify URL hits base_url directly without mount_point.
mockable: httpx transport layer (via respx.mock returning 200 with {status: ok})
expected_effects: GET request is sent to exactly 'https://api.example.com/healthz' (no mount_point, no double slashes).
